1.1: The vision of Obadiah. This is what the Lord GOD says about Edom: We have heard a report from the LORD, and a herald has been sent among the nations: 'Rise up! Let us go to war against her!'
1.2: Look — I have made you the least among the nations; you are utterly despised.
1.3: The pride of your heart has deceived you — you who live in the clefts of the rock, who make your home on the heights, saying to yourself, 'Who can bring me down to earth?'
1.4: Even if you soar as high as the eagle, even if your nest is set among the stars, from there I will bring you down — declares the LORD.
1.5: If thieves came to you — robbers by night — how you have been ruined! — would they not steal only what they wanted? If grape-gatherers came to you, would they not leave some gleanings?
1.6: How thoroughly Esau has been ransacked! His hidden stores laid bare!
1.7: All your treaty-allies have driven you to the border; those at peace with you have deceived you and overpowered you; those who shared your food have set a snare beneath you — there is no discernment left in Edom.
1.8: Will I not on that day — declares the LORD — destroy the wise men from Edom and all understanding from the mount of Esau?
1.9: Your warriors will be shattered, O Teman, so that every man in the mount of Esau will be cut off by slaughter.
1.10: Because of the violence done to your brother Jacob, shame shall cover you, and you shall be cut off forever.
1.11: On the day you stood aside, when strangers carried off his wealth and foreigners entered his gates and cast lots over Jerusalem — you were like one of them.
1.12: You should not have gloated over your brother on the day of his misfortune; you should not have rejoiced over the people of Judah on the day of their ruin; you should not have spoken so arrogantly on the day of their distress.
1.13: You should not have entered the gate of my people on the day of their calamity; you should not have looked on their disaster on the day of their calamity; you should not have seized their goods on the day of their calamity.
1.14: You should not have stationed yourself at the crossroads to cut off his escapees; you should not have handed over his survivors on the day of distress.
1.15: For the day of the LORD is near for all the nations. As you have done, it shall be done to you; your deeds will return upon your own head.
1.16: For just as you drank on my holy mountain, so all the nations shall drink without end; they shall drink and stagger, and be as though they had never existed.
1.17: But on Mount Zion there shall be a delivered remnant, and it shall be holy; and the house of Jacob shall take back what is theirs.
1.18: The house of Jacob will be a fire, and the house of Joseph a flame, but the house of Esau will be stubble; they will set it ablaze and consume it, and no survivor will remain of the house of Esau — for the LORD has spoken.
1.19: The people of the Negev will possess the mount of Esau, and those of the Shephelah will possess the land of the Philistines; they will possess the fields of Ephraim and the fields of Samaria, and Benjamin will possess Gilead.
1.20: The exiles of Israel's forces will possess the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath, and the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad will possess the cities of the Negev.
1.21: Then deliverers will go up to Mount Zion to govern the mount of Esau, and the kingdom will be the LORD's.
